differential_diagnoses: Hemobilia secondary to pseudoaneurysm or arteriobiliary fistula (post-surgical)
Portal hypertensive gastropathy due to cavernous transformation or chronic portal vein thrombosis
Hepatic portal venous system thrombosis with secondary biliary complications
Occult gastrointestinal neoplasm (e.g., small bowel tumor missed by capsule)
next_steps: Selective hepatic artery and mesenteric angiography by Interventional Radiology to identify potential arteriobiliary fistula or pseudoaneurysm
Repeat upper endoscopy with focus on potential varices or portal hypertensive gastropathy, potentially using push enteroscopy
Serum tumor markers (CA 19-9, CEA) and repeat cross-sectional imaging (contrast-enhanced MRI/MRCP) to assess for mass lesion
Endoscopic Ultrasound (EUS) to evaluate perihilar region and vascular structures
Interdisciplinary consultation with Hepatobiliary Surgery regarding the history of hepaticojejunostomy and current vascular findings
